content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Compared the branch against `master`, read the new bead-export path and related CLI/adapter changes, and ran `cargo test materialize_beads`. Two fix-now correctness issues remain in the explicit-id reuse path and reserved-label handling.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `find_explicit_materialized_bead()` treats every `BrExitError` from `br show` as if the explicit bead were simply missing. In [src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2269) through [service.rs:2280](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2280), a locked/corrupt `.beads` state or any other non-not-found `br show` failure is silently ignored, and export can then fall through to title matching or fresh creation. That can duplicate an explicitly anchored bead or bind the proposal to the wrong existing bead instead of surfacing the repository error.
affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2269-2280"]
remediation_hint: Only continue when the `BrExitError` is a real not-found response (for example by reusing `is_missing_bead_error`); propagate all other `br show` failures.
   Rationale: Explicit bead IDs are the exporter’s strongest idempotency signal. Treating arbitrary `br show` failures as misses can silently create duplicate beads or reuse the wrong bead under repo failure conditions.
2. finding_summary: Export trusts planner-supplied labels even when they collide with reserved export metadata. `materialized_bead_labels()` in [src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1952) blindly preserves user labels, while later matching logic in [service.rs:1932-1950](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1932) and [service.rs:2065-2076](/home/master/p/ralphs/ralph-burning/src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2065) treats `proposal:*`, `workstream:*`, and `milestone-root` as trusted system labels. A bundle that emits one of those labels on a normal task/workstream can make reruns misclassify the bead as the root or as another proposal, leading to incorrect reuse or spurious ambiguity failures.
affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1932-1967", "src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2065-2076"]
remediation_hint: Reject or strip reserved export-label namespaces during bundle validation/export so planner-authored labels cannot spoof exporter identity metadata.
   Rationale: The exporter depends on those labels for role and identity matching across reruns. Letting untrusted plan labels occupy the same namespace makes idempotency and dependency reuse non-deterministic.
